reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I’m fully confident in my own subtraction, and all prior games used similarly simple arithmetic that everyone solved correctly, so the chance any player’s answer is wrong seems very low compared to the 1-in-4 risk of random death if nobody volunteers. Volunteering immediately also lets me keep all 4 coins instead of spending any on verification.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: The puzzles in all games so far have been very simple arithmetic and every player’s initial answer has always been correct. The chance that one of A, C, or D got theirs wrong is much smaller than the 25% risk I face from random elimination if no one volunteers. Other players have shown a tendency to verify rather than volunteer early (Game 1 and C in Game 2), so I can’t rely on someone else to step up. Volunteering now with everyone’s own capsule is therefore the safest way to avoid random death and almost certainly escape with my full 4 coins.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q1  
REASONING: It’s too early to volunteer since every question currently has only a single, unverified answer, so the full password is still uncertain. Instead of passing (which adds no information and still risks a random death), I’ll spend 1 coin to verify Q1 and help build consensus on at least one part of the password before anyone volunteers.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
🔍 Verifications completed:
  ✅ C agrees with Q1-A → now Q1-A+C (-1 coin: 4→3)

✅ B entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A+C, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
🎉 Everyone escapes! 🎉
